docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All endpoints (except GET /) require an API key passed in the X-API-Key request header.

docs.title: Configuring keys
docs.text:
Set the API_KEYS environment variable to a comma-separated list of valid keys:

API_KEYS=key-one,key-two,key-three

If API_KEYS is not set, the server generates a random key at startup and logs it as a warning — useful for local development, not for production.

docs.title: Example request
docs.text: curl -H "X-API-Key: your-key" https://your-domain.com/health

docs.title: Error responses
docs.text: Status Meaning 401 Key missing or not recognised

docs.title: Required GitHub Secrets
docs.text:
Go to your repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions and add:

Secret Example Description VPS_HOST 123.45.67.89 VPS IP or hostname VPS_USER ubuntu SSH username VPS_SSH_KEY (private key contents) SSH private key (no passphrase) VPS_PORT 22 SSH port (optional, defaults to 22) APP_DIR /opt/network-monitor Absolute path to the repo on the VPS

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. A push to main triggers the CI workflow first — tests must pass.
  2. If tests pass, the Deploy workflow SSHs into the VPS and runs: 
    git pull origin main
docker compose up -d --build --remove-orphans
docker image prune -f
  3. The concurrency: production lock prevents two deploys racing each other.

docs.title: VPS first-time setup
docs.text:
# On your VPS
git clone https://github.com/<you>/<repo>.git /opt/network-monitor
cd /opt/network-monitor
cp .env.example .env   # fill in real values
docker compose up -d --build

After that, every push to main deploys automatically.

docs.title: POST /api/network-data
docs.text:
Ingest a single sensor reading.

Auth required: Yes

docs.title: Request body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description source string (1–50 chars) ✅ Device identifier timestamp ISO-8601 string — Defaults to server time if omitted latitude float [−90, 90] — longitude float [−180, 180] — altitude float — Metres rsrp int — Reference Signal Received Power (dBm) rsrq int — Reference Signal Received Quality rssi int — Received Signal Strength Indicator level int — Android signal level (0–4) asu int — Arbitrary Strength Unit networkType string (max 20) — e.g. LTE, NR operator string (max 100) — cellId string (max 100) — physicalCellId int — trackingAreaCode int — country string (max 100) — city string (max 100) —

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://your-domain.com/api/network-data \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "source": "device-001",
    "latitude": 51.5074,
    "longitude": -0.1278,
    "rsrp": -85,
    "rsrq": -10,
    "networkType": "LTE",
    "operator": "EE"
  }'

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text: { "message": "Data saved successfully", "id": 42 }

docs.title: POST /api/network-data/batch
docs.text:
Ingest up to 100 readings in a single request.

Auth required: Yes

Each reading is validated independently. The valid set is committed in one transaction; a commit failure marks all readings in the batch as failed. Per-reading validation failures do not abort the rest.

docs.title: Request body
docs.text:
{
  "readings": [
    { "source": "device-001", "rsrp": -85 },
    { "source": "device-002", "rsrp": -102 }
  ]
}

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text:
{
  "total_submitted": 2,
  "successful": 2,
  "failed": 0,
  "details": [
    { "index": 0, "source": "device-001", "status": "success", "id": 43 },
    { "index": 1, "source": "device-002", "status": "success", "id": 44 }
  ]
}

docs.title: Mobile Analytics Endpoints
docs.text:
All mobile endpoints share the same set of optional query parameters:

Parameter Type Description operator string Filter by network operator name network_type string Filter by technology, e.g. LTE, NR period 24h | week | month Restrict to a recent time window source string Filter by device source; all disables the filter lat + lon + radius_km float Geo-filter — all three required together

docs.title: GET /api/mobile/overview
docs.text:
Aggregate signal statistics for the filtered dataset.

Auth required: Yes

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text: Field Type Description mean_rsrp float | null Average RSRP across matching samples mean_rsrq float | null Average RSRQ across matching samples coverage_quality_percent float | null % of samples with RSRP ≥ −100 dBm measurements_count int Total matching samples density_score float | null Samples per km² (only when radius_km is supplied)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl "https://your-domain.com/api/mobile/overview?operator=EE&period=week" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-key"

docs.title: GET /api/mobile/map
docs.text:
Geo-located signal samples for map rendering.

Auth required: Yes

Points are deduplicated by rounding to a ~110 m grid cell (3 decimal places) and averaging RSRP/RSRQ per cell. Maximum 5 000 points returned.

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text:
{
  "points": [
    { "latitude": 51.507, "longitude": -0.128, "rsrp": -85, "rsrq": -10 }
  ]
}

docs.title: GET /api/mobile/trends
docs.text:
Time-series of mean RSRP / RSRQ.

Auth required: Yes

Bucket size depends on the period parameter:

period Bucket 24h (default) 1 hour week 1 day month 1 day

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text:
{
  "points": [
    { "timestamp": "2024-06-01T11:00:00Z", "mean_rsrp": -87.3, "mean_rsrq": -11.2 },
    { "timestamp": "2024-06-01T12:00:00Z", "mean_rsrp": -84.1, "mean_rsrq": -9.8 }
  ]
}

docs.title: GET /
docs.text:
Ping endpoint. No authentication required.

Response

{
  "message": "Network Monitor API is running!",
  "status": "OK",
  "version": "2.0.0"
}

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Deep health check. Verifies the database connection.

Auth required: Yes

Response 200

{
  "status": "healthy",
  "database": "connected",
  "timestamp": "2024-06-01T12:00:00.000000"
}

Response 503 — database unreachable

{ "detail": "Database connection failed" }
